findings.title: Test code in production: 1 issue
findings.description:
Test utilities and patterns found in what appears to be production code.

**Found:**
• Mock/stub objects (line 102)
⚠️ This file is imported by production code!
findings.suggested_fix:
Options:

1. **Move to test files:** If this is test code, move it to a test directory
2. **Use TYPE_CHECKING:** For type hints only, wrap imports in `if TYPE_CHECKING:`
3. **Environment check:** If needed at runtime, guard with environment variable

```python
# Instead of:
from unittest.mock import Mock

# Use:
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
if TYPE_CHECKING:
from unittest.mock import Mock
```
findings.why_it_matters: This test code is imported by production code. Test dependencies add bloat, security risks, and can cause unexpected behavior in production.

findings.title: Mutual recursion: 6 functions in call cycle (complexity 22)
findings.description: Mutual recursion detected: 6 functions form a call cycle. Cycle: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/chi/middleware/route_headers.go::(HeaderRouter).Handler:77 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/chi/middleware/value.go::WithValue:9 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/chi/mux.go::(*Mux).Get:155 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/chi/mux.go::(*Mux).Put:185 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/chi/mux.go::(*Mux).ServeHTTP:63 -> /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/chi/mux.go::(*Mux).handle:416. Aggregate complexity: 22.
findings.suggested_fix: Break the cycle by extracting shared logic into a common helper, introducing an event system, or restructuring the call chain to be unidirectional.
findings.why_it_matters: Mutual recursion creates tight coupling between functions, making them impossible to understand, test, or refactor independently. It can also cause stack overflows if the recursion depth is unbounded.

findings.title: Hardcoded timeout: 2 seconds
findings.description:
Magic timeout value `2500` makes configuration and tuning difficult.

**Analysis:**
⏱️ Duration: 2 seconds
📍 Context: General timeout
📦 In function: `main`
findings.suggested_fix:
Extract to a named constant:

```python
TIMEOUT_2500MS = 2500  # 2 seconds
```
findings.why_it_matters: Hardcoded timeouts are hard to find, tune, and change across environments. Network timeouts especially need to be configurable based on deployment.
